item.title: We need RSS for sharing abundant vibe-coded apps 摘要
item.description:
Matt Webb proposes an RSS feed for sharing vibe-coded apps, comparing app distribution to blogging. Inspired, Simon Willison used Claude to add an Atom feed to his tools page, which aggregates content from his tools.simonwillison.net site. The idea is that as AI accelerates app development, apps become more personal and frequent, making RSS a natural distribution method.
标签: RSS, Atom, vibe-coding, AI, app distribution, web feeds, personal tools, micro-apps, blogging, Claude

item.title: Quoting Andrew Kelley 摘要
item.description:
Andrew Kelley, creator of Zig, argues that it's a misconception that LLM-assisted contributions are undetectable. He states that human mistakes differ fundamentally from LLM hallucinations, making them easy to spot. Additionally, those who use agentic coding have a distinct 'digital smell' that is obvious to non-users. He compares it to a smoker entering a room—non-smokers instantly know. Kelley asserts the right to ban LLM-assisted contributions in his project, emphasizing that while he doesn't forbid using LLMs elsewhere, they are not welcome in Zig's repository.
标签: zig, llms, ai, generative-ai, open-source, code-review, contributor-poker, agentic-coding

item.title: Our evaluation of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 cyber capabilities 摘要
item.description:
The UK's AI Security Institute evaluated OpenAI's GPT-5.5 for cyber capabilities, specifically its ability to find security vulnerabilities. The evaluation found GPT-5.5 to be comparable to Claude Mythos, which was previously assessed by the same institute. However, a key difference is that GPT-5.5 is generally available now, whereas Mythos is not. This raises concerns about the immediate accessibility of advanced AI cyber capabilities.
标签: AI, OpenAI, GPT-5.5, cyber security, vulnerability detection, AI Security Institute, Claude Mythos, Anthropic, LLMs, generative AI
